insight:
Yesterday was a solid rest day with your body showing excellent recovery signs! You burned 2,399 calories despite no formal training, and your resting heart rate of 44 bpm demonstrates fantastic cardiovascular fitness. The low stress level of 30/100 indicates you're handling the pre-race period well mentally.

Your HRV of 118ms is actually above your normal baseline of 93-116ms, which is brilliant news this close to race day. This elevated HRV combined with good sleep quality means your nervous system is recovered and primed. With just 3 days until Chester, stick to your planned light sessions - your body is telling you it's ready to perform.

Focus on boosting your sleep tonight since 6.7 hours wasn't quite optimal for race prep. Aim for 7-8 hours and consider adding an extra 25g protein today to support final recovery phases - your elevated HRV suggests your body is actively repairing and strengthening.
